summary: OBSOLETE / subsumed by EXP-407 SEC-4.2.7a penumbra-scaling correction (decider patch 2026-05-17T01-23Z). ISS-1753 was the SEC-4.2.7a-EXP-276.c2 H1 finding that footnote (i) asserted R_p ∝ h_Moon (linear scaling, which holds only when h_Sun and h_Moon scale together). EXP-407 (the penumbra-scaling correction that fixed ISS-1643) rewrote that footnote with an exact two-reading analysis (wholesale rescaling vs partial scaling), making the original linearity-criticism moot — the new text no longer claims R_p ∝ h_Moon. Recommend close as obsolete; no further analyst action required.
verification: monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json ISS-1643 fix_details: "EXP-407: footnote (i) in SEC-4.2.7a replaced with exact two-reading analysis (wholesale rescaling vs partial scaling). Applied by decider decider-2026-05-17T01-23Z". The footnote-rewrite addresses ISS-1753's parent claim.
